notes: Scullin et al. 2018, Journal of Experimental Psychology: General; n=57 RCT with polysomnography; to-do list writing cut sleep onset by 9 min, d=0.63. Small sample but well-controlled.
PubMed abstract for Scullin et al. 2018 — same study; confirms key findings. Listed as backup citation.
2024 PMC survey of bedtime routines in college students; identifies planning/list-writing as effective strategy for sleep onset; self-report data, not RCT
Sleep Foundation consumer guidance on bedtime routines; accurately reflects CBT-I wind-down protocol; reputable org but not a primary study
